name: Pork Fried Rice
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups pork shoulder, roast cut into cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the pork shoulder into cubes and place in a freezer bag. Freeze.
2. To prepare: thaw the pork cubes before cooking.
3. Heat the salad oil in a wok until hot. Carefully add the cubed pork.
4. Stir-fry the pork until completely cooked. Remove the pork from the wok and set aside.
5. Add the onions, cabbage, and bean sprouts to the remaining oil in the wok. Stir-fry just until tender-crisp.
6. Remove the vegetables from the wok and set with the pork.
7. Add the cooked rice to the remaining oil in the wok (add 1-2 tablespoons of oil if necessary). Fry lightly until each grain is separate, coated, and hot.
8. Return the vegetables and pork to the wok and mix with the rice until hot.
9. Add the sesame oil and soy sauce to taste.
10. Push the rice mixture up the sides of the wok and add the beaten egg to the bottom. Cook and stir until set.
11. Toss the egg lightly with the fried rice; serve hot.
Serving Ideas: Hot and Sour Soup, Potstickers, Fortune Cookies, Green Tea
